title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Today, I'm going to show you guys how to order at these restaurants with confidence.
I'm going to add the queso sauce.
That's what I'm going to get to.
He's going to get it, I think.
I've got two drinks in mind that I'm going to order today.

title: will / shall
examples: After watching this video, you'll be able to order Starbucks like a Pro.
I'll have the brown rice.
I'll do a bowl with chicken for here.
I'll do the white rice.
I will do the green salsa.

title: will / won't
examples: No, I won't do queso.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: So maybe you can research ahead of time and see what the menu is before you go to the restaurant.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: After watching this video, you'll be able to order Starbucks like a Pro.
In the US, there are many fast food restaurants where you can customize your order, which is really great because then you can get it tailored exactly to your tastes.
This is my mom's first time ever to Chipotle so you can see a total newbie ordering.
You can do it.
I can do it.

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: It may be a carrier.
I never order Frappuccinos at Starbucks, but I might start after trying this.

title: have to / must
examples: I do, but sometimes I avoid them, because you get nervous, you don't know what they have to offer and everything.
They just cut them up in strips that you have to do the breast.

title: should (advice)
examples: And that should be good.
Just a tall, should be okay.
And that should do it.
A 20 should be okay.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: Would you like to add the garlic?
I would like a drink.
Would you want a tray for that?
And for my other drink, I would like a Grande iced matcha with two pumps of pistachio and chocolate cold foam.
The first thing would you like tonight?

title: If I do / If I did
examples: I don't know if they have this in Korea, but this thing is huge.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: The reason why they call this the Squid Games Frappuccino is because the color of it is supposed to look like the color of the uniforms that they wear.
Don't be embarrassed to ask for help.

title: have something done
examples: Can I have a little bit of a lettuce, please?
I have a little bit of a complicated order today.
I'll also do a six-inch, you have the chicken breast.
No, thank you, but I'll have the chicken heated as well.
